added_rules.content: When implementing scheduler or timer-based services, include idempotency guards using unique constraint or dedup key to prevent duplicate processing on retick
When receiving STOP or URGENT mail from the orchestrator, immediately cease implementation, commit any partial progress, and release the bead
When implementing event-driven workflows, test the full emit-to-consume-to-side-effect path in E2E tests rather than testing emitter and consumer separately
When a bead lists files_to_create and files_to_modify, treat those as the authoritative scope — do not expand scope beyond listed files without creating a child bead
When a user rejects or interrupts a tool use, stop the current approach entirely and wait for new direction rather than retrying the same action
